what_it_does: Defines a component template for a current-to-solution layout with title, main, and footer zones; a split main area; left problem plate, connector, and right solution plate slots; styling tokens; dials; invariants; and context metadata.
observations: The template id is component.template.current-to-solution and its type is template.
The zones are title, main, and footer with proportions 0.14, 0.72, and 0.1.
The main zone uses a split layout with ratio [0.46, "connector", 0.46].
The left card slot is a rich problem plate on a nested surface, and the right card slot is a spacious solution plate on a warm surface.
The connector slot accepts component.molecule.movement-connector and uses dashed styling with a plug terminal.
The context relation explicitly says the dashed plug between plates is the movement layer.
standouts: The connector is treated as the movement layer rather than just a visual separator.
The template encodes a before-to-after relationship through asymmetric card roles and surfaces.
The invariants constrain color, title treatment, and focal hierarchy.
conventions: Uses brace-wrapped design token references for surfaces and styling values.
Uses role, accepts, and into fields to describe placement and semantic intent.
Uses short string dials and invariants as declarative configuration.
Uses provenance text to explain measured layout proportions.
concerns: The listed zone proportions total 0.96, leaving 0.04 not represented by the zone entries.
The main zone accepts organism while the connector slot placed into main.center accepts a molecule component; the nesting rule is not explained in this file.
notes: Brief this as a declarative before-after template where two plates are connected by an explicit movement connector.
questions: Is the missing 0.04 of zone proportion intentional spacing or an omitted zone?
How should the main zone acceptance rule interact with the connector slot accepting a molecule component?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated description and relation match the actual structure: a problem plate and solution plate connected by a dashed plug movement layer.
intention_signals: It seems intended to preserve a calm before-after composition where movement is carried by the connector and the two plates have distinct density and surface treatments.
novelty: The file explicitly names the connector itself as the movement layer within a layout template.
contribution: Contributes a declarative current-to-solution substrate with semantic zones, asymmetric plate roles, a movement connector, styling tokens, dials, invariants, and provenance that a fused system could retain as a reusable transition layout pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON component template defining a before-after current-to-solution layout with title, split main plates, dashed plug connector, footer, styling tokens, dials, invariants, and context metadata.
intention_for_embedding: This was for encoding a controlled visual transition from a problem/current state to a solution state through an explicit connector movement layer.
